title: Simple Modern Bar Stool
summary: A small, sturdy bar stool with front legs splayed 5° for stability — basic lumber and screws-from-underneath assembly, no jigs required.
description:
Bar stools live or die on base support — a stool with straight legs and no stretchers feels tippy the moment someone leans on it. This design solves that with front legs splayed just 5° off vertical (barely visible, but enough to widen the stance at the floor) and a full box of aprons and leg supports tying everything together underneath the seat.

Build both leg assemblies identically before final assembly, and screw the base to the seat from underneath so no fasteners show on top — the visible seat surface is the one place on this build where a rough hole or filled screw would actually be noticeable.

tools.note: Cuts the 5° angled front legs and diagonal leg pieces.

cutList.note: Both ends cut 5° off square, parallel to each other.
Longest point measurement, one end cut at 5° off square.
Longest point measurement, one end cut at 5° off square.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut one seat at 11-1/2" from the 1x12. Cut two short aprons at 11-1/2" and two longer aprons at 13" from the 1x4. Cut two back legs at 30", two front legs at 30-1/8", one diagonal leg brace, long at 12-5/8", one diagonal leg brace, short at 10-1/8" and two leg supports at 13" from the 2x2. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to one 5 ft 1x4 and two 8 ft 2x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Attach the seat aprons
steps.body: Attach the short seat aprons to the seat with 2-1/2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the longer aprons
steps.body: Attach the longer aprons to both the seat and the shorter aprons with screws and glue, boxing the underside of the seat.

steps.title: Build the leg assemblies
steps.body: Build two matching leg assemblies from the front legs, back legs, diagonal braces, and leg supports, using 2-1/2" screws and glue at every joint. Keep the assemblies as close to identical as you can — any mismatch will make the stool rock.

steps.title: Assemble and finish
steps.body: Assemble the seat and both leg assemblies, screwing up from underneath the seat so no screw holes show on top. Sand every edge and finish as desired.

images.alt: Simple modern bar stool with splayed front legs
